stimulus.content: Both the House and the Senate have passed the same version of a bill. The bill is now sent to the President. The President reads the bill and decides what to do next.

stem: What happens if the President signs the bill?

choices.text: It goes back to the Senate for changes
It is sent to the Supreme Court for review
It becomes a law for the whole country
It is voted on by the citizens next

answer_explanation: When the President signs a bill that has been passed by both the House and Senate, it becomes a law. The bill does not go back to Congress or to the Supreme Court. Citizens do not vote on bills in the national lawmaking process.
